subject: Public Health
title: Hygiene in Healthcare Settings
description: A quick, practical guide to Hygiene in Healthcare Settings.

hook.text: One in every four health facilities in developing countries has no basic water service—putting patients at risk.

buildup.text: Healthcare-associated infections kill hundreds of thousands annually—many from poor hand hygiene.

discovery.text: Ignaz Semmelweis showed in 1847 that handwashing between patients cut maternal death rates from 18% to 2%.

twist.text: Even today, compliance with hand hygiene in hospitals averages only 40% worldwide.

climax.text: The WHO's Save Lives: Clean Your Hands campaign has improved compliance in hospitals across 190 countries.

punchline.text: Hospitals should heal—not infect. Hand hygiene is the first defense.

quiz.question: What is the average hand hygiene compliance rate in hospitals worldwide?
